---
title: 'Six (6) Parenting Lessons I Learned with my budding 14-year-old Entrepreneur.'
date: '2019-11-28T08:14:00.0020000+00:00'
draft: false
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '1693934432626629868'
blogger_filename: '/2019/11/six-6-parenting-lessons-i-learned-with.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Musings'
---

<h3>
<b>Pokemon Cards created a Teachable Moment in Entrepreneurship.&nbsp;</b></h3>
<br />
<table align="center" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="tr-caption-container" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; text-align: center;"><tbody>
<tr><td style="text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/facebook-ads-e9fbfb5397ca.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><img border="0" data-original-height="236" data-original-width="538" height="175" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/facebook-ads-5b306d8244e5.jpg" width="400" /></a></td></tr>
<tr><td class="tr-caption" style="text-align: center;">Photo DarrellWolfe via Facebook Marketplace (my personal ad)</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<br />
<h4>
<b>Parenting Lessons: How Pokemon Cards created a Teachable Moment in Entrepreneurship</b></h4>
<br />
Not too very long ago, my 14-year-old son asked me to help him do something he could not do for himself: Sell Pokemon Cards on Facebook Marketplace.<br />
<br />
We all played for a few years, collecting a few more each release, even going to events. After his mom died, he kept playing but his brother did not.<br />
<br />
He inherited his late mom's cards, then his brother gave up playing and gave his, then I gave up mine... by the end, he had well in excess of 7,000 extra cards (those not in his current playing decks or collections).<br />
<br />
He asked permission to sell them, I suggested Facebook Marketplace. He diligently separated them into categories, grouping them in fair sets. Eventually, he needed my help listing them.<br />
<br />
Since he is in school now and doesn't own a car or driver's license yet, I found myself responding to his ads and taking the cards and handing them off and collecting money.<br />
<br />
As I look back on the last months, I have several things to glean from the experience.<br />
<br />
<h4>
<b>Lessons in Parenting: What went well.</b></h4>
<br />
<br />
<ol>
<li><b>Place the ownership on them. </b>Make them do as much as they can. Encouraging him to do all the pre-work allowed him to take responsibility for the quality of his own product and make a better sale.&nbsp;</li>
<li><b>Encourage their diligence. </b>I'm darn proud of him staying on top of the process, checking to see if there have been messages from buyers, and asking for feedback.&nbsp;</li>
<li><b>Teach them to create value. </b>He sold two very quickly, both to parents who used to play and are now teaching their kids. Both parents agreed that it was a fair deal, and great assortment. He did a great job organizing them in a way that was fair to the buyer and gave good value. He didn't need to do anything other than organizing them in a special way and sell that organization to create that value.&nbsp;</li>
</ol>
<br />
<h4>
<b>Lessons in Parenting: Opportunities for growth.</b></h4>
<br />
<ol>
<li><b>Walk them all the way through.</b> I could have opted to wait to schedule a time to sell the cards to ensure he would be there to meet the buyers. I didn't do that with the first one but I did with the second. That gave him the opportunity to handle the sale and meet buyers face to face.&nbsp;</li>
<li><b>Your Engagement is Crucial</b>: I could have been more engaged with him in the process. I treated it like an afterthought and didn't give him the same priority he showed the project. I doubled back to re-encourage him and tell him what a great job he did and was doing with the project.</li>
<li><b>Setbacks are inevitable.</b> The rest didn't sell as quickly. He did some work to try to get them listed on Amazon and that hit a roadblock. So we learned that we could double back and get a new plan. So we paid for $1 worth of advertising to boost the listing on Facebook Marketplace.&nbsp;</li>
</ol>
<br />
<br />
<div id="mc_embed_signup">
<form action="https://darrellwolfe.us3.list-manage.com/subscribe/post?u=979cb0db6ca74343e82a059ba&amp;id=14df3ba97b" class="validate" id="mc-embedded-subscribe-form" method="post" name="mc-embedded-subscribe-form" novalidate="" target="_blank">
<div id="mc_embed_signup_scroll">
<label for="mce-EMAIL">Subscribe Now - Pursue a No Hiding Lifestyle</label>&nbsp;<input class="email" id="mce-EMAIL" name="EMAIL" placeholder="email address" required="" type="email" value="" /><br />
<div aria-hidden="true" style="left: -5000px; position: absolute;">
<input name="b_979cb0db6ca74343e82a059ba_14df3ba97b" tabindex="-1" type="text" value="" /></div>
<div class="clear">
<input class="button" id="mc-embedded-subscribe" name="subscribe" type="submit" value="Subscribe" /></div>
</div>
</form>
</div>
<br />
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;">Shalom: Live Long and Prosper!</span><br />
<div class="MsoNormal">
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;; font-size: 18pt;">Darrell Wolfe (DG Wolfe)</span></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;">Storyteller | Writer | Thinker | Consultant @&nbsp;<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/start-here-about_12.html" target="_blank">DarrellWolfe.com</a></span><br />
<br />
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;"><a href="https://www.gallupstrengthscenter.com/" target="_blank">Clifton StrengthsFinder</a>: Intellection, Learner,&nbsp;</span><span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;">Ideation,</span><span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;">&nbsp;Achiever, Input</span><br />
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;"><a href="https://www.16personalities.com/infj-personality" target="_blank">16Personalities</a> (Myers-Briggs Type): INFJ</span><br />
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;"><br /></span>
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;"><br /></span></div>
